page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.2xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in single-threaded data operations, with gzip compression reaching 36.56 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression achieving 17.53 GB/sec. Memory subsystem performance is also strong, as reflected in a top-tier PassMark memory score of 4334.73. However, the architecture exhibits a relative weakness in cryptographic operations, scoring in the bottom 25% on the PassMark CPU encryption test. Large-model LLM prompt processing is also limited, with the medium 7B model processing prompts at 22.61 tokens/sec, though text generation for smaller 135M models reaches a top-tier rate of 393.87 tokens/sec.

The inclusion of dedicated physical cores and local NVMe SSD storage offers a resource-dense profile for general-purpose workloads. This hardware configuration is suitable for database operations, static web serving, and single-threaded compression tasks. The absence of hyperthreading and the presence of local instance storage make it a reliable choice for workloads requiring predictable CPU execution and fast local scratch space, though it is less suited for heavy cryptographic or large-scale machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.2xlarge is a general-purpose server utilizing an 8-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor with a 2.8 GHz clock speed and 8 dedicated physical cores. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, a 474 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression performance, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score. However, it shows weaker performance in cryptographic operations, scoring in the bottom 25% on the PassMark CPU encryption test. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, static web serving, and data compression tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores and fast local NVMe storage, while avoiding heavy cryptographic or large-scale LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.2xlarge is an 8-core ARMv9 general-purpose server featuring 32 GB RAM, a 474 GB NVMe SSD, and top-tier single-threaded compression and memory benchmark performance.

tagline: An ARMv9 general-purpose server featuring dedicated physical cores, integrated NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier single-threaded data compression performance.

32.0 GB of system memory paired with a 474 GB local NVMe SSD
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds
Weaker performance in cryptographic operations and medium-model LLM prompt processing
Suitable for database operations, static web serving, and data compression tasks
